title: Noor's Bouncy Ball Blast
synopsis: When Noor's bouncy ball won't stop bouncing higher and higher, she teams up with her diverse group of friends to discover the science behind bouncing and find a silly solution to bring her favorite toy back down to earth.

pages.text: Noor loved her new bouncy ball. It was bright purple with sparkly silver stars. "Watch this!" she said to her friend Marcus. Noor dropped the ball on the playground. BOING! It bounced back up to her hand. "Cool!" said Marcus.

illustration.description: Noor, a young girl with dark curly hair and bright brown eyes, holds a glittery purple bouncy ball with silver stars. She's smiling excitedly at Marcus, a boy with light brown skin and short black hair, on a colorful school playground. The sun shines brightly overhead with puffy white clouds.
illustration.alt: Noor showing her bouncy ball to Marcus on the playground

pages.text: "Let me try!" said Aisha, running over with her red hijab flowing. Noor tossed the ball to Aisha. Aisha dropped it from higher up. BOING! BOING! The ball bounced twice as high! "Whoa!" they all said together.

illustration.description: Aisha, a girl wearing a bright red hijab with kind dark eyes, catches the purple bouncy ball as it soars high above her head. Noor and Marcus watch with amazed expressions, their mouths open in surprise. The playground equipment is visible in the background with vibrant primary colors.
illustration.alt: Aisha catching the bouncy ball as it bounces very high

pages.text: "My turn!" shouted Diego, adjusting his glasses. He climbed up on the monkey bars. Diego dropped the ball from way up high. BOING! BOING! BOING! The ball bounced higher than the swings! "Uh oh," said Noor. "Something weird is happening."

illustration.description: Diego, a boy with brown skin, black hair, and round glasses, hangs from colorful monkey bars looking worried. Below him, the purple ball bounces incredibly high, almost reaching the swing set. Noor, Marcus, and Aisha stand below with concerned but curious expressions.
illustration.alt: Diego on monkey bars as the ball bounces extremely high

pages.text: The bouncy ball kept bouncing higher and higher! Soon it was bouncing over the slide. Then over the trees! "Come back!" called Noor. But the ball just kept bouncing up, up, up! Mrs. Chen, their teacher, came running over.

illustration.description: The purple bouncy ball with silver stars soars high above tall green trees and a colorful playground slide. Noor reaches up desperately with both arms. Mrs. Chen, an Asian woman with shoulder-length black hair and a friendly face, hurries toward the group of children with a concerned expression.
illustration.alt: The bouncy ball bouncing over trees as Noor and Mrs. Chen watch

pages.text: "What's going on?" asked Mrs. Chen. "My ball won't stop bouncing!" cried Noor. "It's bouncing higher every time!" Mrs. Chen pushed up her glasses and smiled. "This sounds like a science mystery! Let's figure it out together."

illustration.description: Mrs. Chen kneels down to the children's level, her finger thoughtfully on her chin with a detective-like expression. The four children - Noor, Marcus, Aisha, and Diego - gather around her in a circle on the playground, all looking curious and excited about solving the mystery.
illustration.alt: Mrs. Chen and the children discussing the bouncy ball mystery

pages.text: BOING! BOING! BOING! The ball bounced so high they could barely see it! "Look!" pointed Aisha. "It's just a tiny purple dot now!" Marcus giggled. "It looks like a flying grape!" Even Noor had to laugh, but she still wanted her ball back.

illustration.description: All five characters look up at the bright blue sky, shading their eyes with their hands. High above, a tiny purple speck is barely visible among fluffy white clouds. The children have amazed and amused expressions, with Marcus pointing and laughing while Noor looks worried but is starting to smile.
illustration.alt: Everyone looking up at the tiny bouncy ball high in the sky

pages.text: "I have an idea!" said Mrs. Chen. "What if we make the ball bounce on something soft instead of hard?" Diego's eyes got big behind his glasses. "Like a pillow?" "Exactly!" said Mrs. Chen. "Hard surfaces make balls bounce high. Soft surfaces make them bounce low."

illustration.description: Mrs. Chen holds up one finger with an excited 'eureka' expression. Diego's eyes are wide with understanding behind his round glasses. The other children lean in with interested faces, forming a tight group. Thought bubbles above their heads show pillows and soft materials in bright, cheerful colors.
illustration.alt: Mrs. Chen explaining the science solution to the children

pages.text: "But how do we catch it?" asked Marcus. The ball was still bouncing way up in the clouds! Mrs. Chen winked. "We wait for it to come down. Then we'll be ready with something super soft!" Aisha clapped her hands. "Like my grandmother's quilts!"

illustration.description: Marcus scratches his head looking puzzled while pointing upward. Mrs. Chen winks playfully with her hands on her hips. Aisha jumps excitedly with her red hijab bright against the sky. In the background, the purple speck of the ball is beginning to descend from the puffy white clouds.
illustration.alt: The children planning how to catch the bouncy ball

pages.text: They ran inside and gathered soft things. Pillows from the reading corner! Blankets from the nurse's office! Even the gym teacher, Mr. Rodriguez, brought foam mats. "This should work!" he said with a big smile. His curly mustache wiggled when he talked.

illustration.description: The children and teachers work together carrying colorful pillows, soft blankets, and blue foam mats. Mr. Rodriguez, a man with tan skin, curly dark hair, and a prominent mustache, carries a stack of mats with a cheerful grin. Everyone is rushing with determined, happy expressions toward the playground.
illustration.alt: Everyone gathering soft materials to catch the ball

pages.text: They made a giant soft pile outside. Pillows, blankets, and mats everywhere! "Here it comes!" shouted Noor. The purple ball was falling down, down, down! It was getting bigger and bigger as it got closer.

illustration.description: A huge, colorful pile of soft materials sits in the middle of the playground - fluffy pillows in rainbow colors, cozy patterned blankets, and blue foam mats. The children and teachers stand around it looking up excitedly. The purple ball appears larger now as it falls from the bright sky toward their soft target.
illustration.alt: The soft landing pile ready to catch the falling ball

pages.text: PLOP! The ball landed right in the middle of the soft pile! It barely bounced at all! "Hooray!" cheered everyone. Noor grabbed her ball and hugged it tight. "Welcome back, bouncy ball!" she said. The ball just sat quietly in her hands.

illustration.description: The purple bouncy ball sits nestled gently in the colorful pile of soft materials. Noor kneels beside the pile, holding her ball close to her chest with a relieved, joyful smile. All her friends and teachers cheer around her with raised arms and big grins. Confetti-like sparkles fill the air to show celebration.
illustration.alt: Noor happily reunited with her bouncy ball

pages.text: "Now I know the secret!" said Noor. "Hard surfaces make balls bounce high. Soft surfaces make them bounce low!" She gently bounced her ball on the grass. It gave a tiny, perfect bounce. "Just right!" laughed Noor. Her friends all wanted to try bouncing their toys on soft things too!

illustration.description: Noor sits cross-legged on the green grass, gently bouncing her purple ball with a satisfied smile. Her diverse group of friends sit in a circle around her, each holding different toys - Marcus has a red rubber ball, Aisha holds a small yellow toy, and Diego has a blue bouncy cube. Everyone looks happy and content in the warm sunshine.
illustration.alt: Noor and friends learning about bouncing on different surfaces

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What color was Noor's bouncy ball?
questions.explanation: Noor's bouncy ball was bright purple with sparkly silver stars!

questions.question: What happened when the ball was dropped from higher places?
questions.explanation: The ball bounced higher and higher each time it was dropped from a higher place!

questions.question: What did Mrs. Chen say makes balls bounce high?
questions.explanation: Mrs. Chen explained that hard surfaces make balls bounce high, while soft surfaces make them bounce low.

questions.question: How did they catch the bouncy ball when it came down?
choices: With a pile of soft things
questions.explanation: They made a giant pile of pillows, blankets, and foam mats to catch the ball softly!

questions.question: Who helped gather the foam mats?
questions.explanation: Mr. Rodriguez, the gym teacher with the curly mustache, brought foam mats to help catch the ball!

pages.instructions: Help complete the story by choosing the right words!
pages.text: Noor's bouncy ball was [0] with silver stars. When they dropped it from high places, it bounced [1] and higher! Mrs. Chen said [2] surfaces make balls bounce high, but [3] surfaces make them bounce low.

pages.title: Science Words
pages.instructions: Choose the best word to complete each sentence about bouncing!
pages.text: The children made a [0] pile to catch the ball. They used pillows, blankets, and [1] mats. When the ball landed, it made a quiet [2] sound instead of a loud BOING!
